column4: And {{c1::Jacob}} sent messengers before him to Esau his brother unto the land of Seir, the field of Edom.
And Jacob sent messengers before him to {{c1::Esau}} his brother unto the land of Seir, the field of Edom.
And he commanded them, saying: ‘{{c1::Thus}} shall ye say unto my lord Esau: Thus saith thy servant Jacob: I have sojourned with Laban, and stayed until now.
And he commanded them, saying: ‘Thus shall ye say unto my lord {{c1::Esau}}: Thus saith thy servant Jacob: I have sojourned with Laban, and stayed until now.

Then {{c1::Jacob}} was greatly afraid and was distressed. And he divided the people that was with him, and the flocks, and the herds, and the camels, into two camps.

I am not worthy of all the mercies, and of all the truth, which {{c1::Thou}} hast shown unto Thy servant; for with my staff I passed over this Jordan; and now I am become two camps.

Deliver me, {{c1::I}} pray Thee, from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esau; for I fear him, lest he come and smite me, the mother with the children.
